description: A story beat — the fundamental narrative building block. Beats select world graph elements and add dramaturgical structure (function, tension, reveals). They form hierarchies via parent references: a top-level beat without parent acts as a storyline container (what was previously an "arc"), while child beats represent individual story moments within that storyline. Vertical chaining (parent) groups beats within a storyline; horizontal chaining (threads) connects beats across storylines by shared thematic meaning.

description.description: What happens in this beat or what this storyline covers

parent.description: ID of parent beat — vertical chaining within a storyline (e.g. main plot > subplots > scenes). A beat without parent is a top-level storyline.

node_ids.description: Node IDs from the world graph relevant to this beat. Parent beats inherit node references from their children — only specify IDs that are not already covered by child beats.

edge_ids.description: Edge IDs from the world graph relevant to this beat. Parent beats inherit edge references from their children — only specify IDs that are not already covered by child beats.

function.description: Dramaturgical function of this beat. All dramaturgical properties (function, reveals, tension, emotional_target, transition) are optional — a beat with only id, name, and world references is valid.

target.description: Node or edge ID being revealed to the reader

reveals.description: Information disclosed to the reader at this beat

tension.description: Tension level at this beat (for tension curve modeling)

emotional_target.description: Target emotional valence at this beat

type.description: How this beat connects to the next

to_beat.description: Beat ID this transitions to (if cross-hierarchy)
